title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make using just lemons and sea salt. This is a traditional salt-curing method where whole lemons are packed with salt and fermented for four weeks, resulting in tangy, umami-rich preserved lemons that add bold flavor to any dish.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters, without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt, filling each lemon with salt until all the salt is used up.
Start packing the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible, and lemon juice will be released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice; if needed, juice one additional lemon and add the juice to the jar.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.

Once opened, store in the fridge indefinitely, rinsing off any excess salt and brine before using the preserved lemons in recipes, and discard the pulp while using the well-rinsed rind anywhere you would use lemon zest.
